text.title: Serverless Cold Start Optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every Lambda gets provisioned concurrency = 5 regardless of traffic.
antipattern.fix: Audit functions; remove PC where memory bump or runtime swap closes the gap; size PC by traffic.

antipattern.symptom: Java Lambda chosen for 200ms p99 budget without SnapStart.
antipattern.fix: Enable SnapStart on JVM + AutoPublishAlias OR swap to Node/Python.

antipattern.symptom: Memory bumped 256→2048MB; warm latency improved 10ms but monthly cost up 7×.
antipattern.root-cause: r5 not applied; cost not weighed.
antipattern.fix: Find smallest memory that meets cold p99; benchmark at 4 levels (256/512/1024/2048).

antipattern.symptom: Lambda bundle 80MB after pulling in unused SDK clients.

root-cause.source: AWS SDK v3 modular import docs.

antipattern.fix: Tree-shake; import only used SDK clients; expect 30-60% cold-start improvement.
